steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student claims IGF-1 gene variations 'must account for' size differences among dog breeds. The question asks which finding, if true, would most directly weaken this causal claim.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'multiple genes known to affect size': If other genes also drive size differences, IGF-1 alone cannot account for all the variation. This directly undermines the claim. ✓
  • Choice B — 'one variant more common in small breeds': This actually supports the idea that IGF-1 variation correlates with size — it does not weaken the claim. ✗
  • Choice C — 'IGF-1 variations do affect size variance': This confirms the student's claim rather than challenging it. ✗
  • Choice D — 'applies to other species': Scope across species is irrelevant to whether IGF-1 alone explains dog size. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
